experiences.intentions: The board is empty and the active piece is an I horizontally near the top. Early game goal is to place pieces cleanly to preserve a flat stack and flexibility for upcoming O, T, S, L pieces. Candidate moves: left/right shift the I toward edges for a good flat placement; rotating vertical is less useful on an empty board because it creates a tall column and reduces flatness; soft_drop/hard_drop now would place it in the current central position, which is acceptable but less ideal than tucking it to a side. Moving left starts positioning it along the wall while keeping options open for later drops.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty and the active I piece is horizontal near the top at columns 2-5. Early game goal is to build a clean stack and ideally place the I piece at an edge to preserve flexibility for upcoming O,T,S,L. Candidate moves: hard_drop would place it immediately where it is, leaving a flat bar but not at an edge; rotate would make it vertical but needs more horizontal repositioning later; soft_drop is too slow. Moving left starts shifting the horizontal I toward the wall for a clean low placement on a later turn.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except a settled O near the top and the active I piece lying horizontally on the bottom row from columns 2-5, leaving a one-cell gap at the far left and space to the right. Immediate threat is minimal; the goal is to place the I efficiently for future flat stacking. Candidate moves: left would shift it into columns 1-4 and fill the left wall gap on the next turns, right shifts away from the wall, rotations would make it vertical and create height, drops are irrelevant since it is already on the floor. Best sub-goal is to tuck it flush to the left for a cleaner base, so choose left.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high at the top center, while the stack below is low and flat except for an O block at columns 2-3 and an I block along the bottom from columns 1-4. There is no immediate danger, so the goal is to place the T efficiently to keep the surface clean and set up future S/L/J/Z pieces. A good sub-goal is moving the T toward the left-side notch above the O/I structure, where a T can fit neatly later. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it poorly on the empty field and waste flexibility; soft_drop commits downward before lateral alignment; rotations are not yet needed because the spawn orientation is flexible enough while moving. Moving left starts aligning toward the useful placement area and preserves options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Key state: a T piece is near the top centered around columns 3-5; the stack at the bottom has I on the bottom row from columns 1-4 and an O block above columns 2-3, leaving the board otherwise open. Goal: place pieces efficiently to keep a flat surface and avoid creating holes. Best near-term setup is to move the T toward the left so it can later fill around the existing left-side structure cleanly; dropping immediately in the center wastes the open field and doesn't build on the stack. Candidate actions: hard/soft drop are premature, rotations are not needed yet on an empty board, right moves away from the existing stack, while left starts aligning the T over the left structure for a compact placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Key state: a T piece is high near the center-left; stack is very low with an O at columns 2-3 above an I floor at row 19 columns 1-4, leaving broad open space elsewhere. No immediate threat from height. Goal: place the T efficiently to keep the surface flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming S,L,J,Z. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the T in its current centered orientation on the flat floor region, likely creating extra bumps over the existing left stack. Moving right first can position it over the wide empty right side and set up a cleaner placement later; moving left crowds the existing stack. Rotations are not urgent because there is abundant time and space. So the best immediate sub-goal is lateral repositioning to the right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty with a flat-ish stack at the bottom: an I on the bottom row from columns 1-4 and an O resting above it at columns 2-3, leaving a low central platform. The active T is high above, currently horizontal. Goal is to place it efficiently to keep the stack flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming S, L, J, Z pieces. Since there is no immediate threat and hard dropping now would place the T around row 15 with its center over columns 3-5, creating a modest bump but still acceptable. Moving right would better center the T over the O block so a future tuck/rotation may be stronger, and with the board empty there is time. Candidate actions: hard_drop commits too early; soft_drop only advances vertically without improving alignment; rotate actions are unnecessary now; left shifts toward the I edge and is less useful. Right improves future placement options while staying safe.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The stack is low and flat except for an O block at columns 2-3 on rows 17-18 and an I block along the bottom at columns 1-4, leaving a clean right side. The active T is high near the center in spawn orientation. Goal is to place it efficiently without creating holes, ideally moving toward the right to set up upcoming S/L/J/Z pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the T near the center on top of the O/I structure, which is acceptable but commits early; rotations are not yet necessary because the board is open; soft_drop is safe but less progress than horizontal positioning. Moving right begins steering the T toward the open right side, preserving flexibility for later rotation/drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Goal is to place L to improve the surface and avoid creating holes before upcoming J,Z,L,I. Current vertical orientation could be useful on the left, but immediate best single action is to move toward the left-side notch/edge where L can later fit cleanly; dropping now would commit too early in the center. Rotating is not urgent because current orientation can still be maneuvered after shifting. Moving left advances toward a cleaner placement option and preserves flexibility.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L high on the left in vertical orientation. Surface below has a low left-side stack (I on bottom left, O/T nearby) and open space; no immediate danger. Goal is to place the L efficiently to build a flat stack and avoid creating holes. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit immediately in current column, likely landing on the far left and not using the central notch well; left/right can adjust horizontally; rotation could prepare a better fit, especially a flat L orientation to sit across uneven terrain. Since the piece is still high, best immediate sub-goal is orient it first. Rotating left from current rotation 1 gives rotation 0, a broad horizontal L that is generally more flexible for landing on the current surface next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper-left with a mostly flat stack low on the board and a small central-right bump from the T/SS pieces. Immediate threats are minimal because the board is very open. The goal now is to place the L to improve the surface and avoid creating overhangs; with next pieces J,Z,L,I, keeping the center accessible and preserving a lane for I is useful. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the current horizontal L high-impact but without fine alignment; left/right could reposition before dropping, but a strong immediate setup is to rotate into vertical orientation first so it can fit more cleanly along the left side on future drops. Soft_drop is slower with no urgent need. Rotating right to orientation 1 gives a compact vertical L that can be moved/dropped into a cleaner slot next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an L in rotation 1 near the upper-left. The stack is low; the main features are a flat-ish floor at the bottom with a left-side I at row 19, O/T cluster around columns 2-6, and an S over columns 4-6. There is no immediate danger, so the goal is to place the L to improve surface smoothness and avoid creating overhangs. The most natural opportunity is to move this L toward the center/right to later fit around the S/T structure, but with only one action now, shifting right advances toward that target while keeping options open. Left would push it against the wall with little benefit, rotations are premature before horizontal alignment, and dropping now would likely commit to a suboptimal left placement high above useful contours.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in rotation 1, currently floating above a mid-right stack. Surface has a left-side low area over the I line and a central/right bump around the T and S pieces. Goal is to place the L to improve the skyline and avoid creating holes while setting up for upcoming J/Z/L/I. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the current vertical L where it stands, likely awkward and high; soft_drop delays commitment without improving position; right moves toward the S/T bump and risks messy stacking. Rotating left to rotation 0 makes the L horizontal with foot on the left, which can fit more cleanly over the left-center area on future drops. Moving left now also starts shifting toward the open left well and keeps options open. Best immediate sub-goal is to reposition toward the left side where the L can fill space above the O/I area. Action: left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current board has a mostly flat low stack with a left-side notch above the I line and some unevenness around the center-right S/T/O cluster. The active piece is an L in rotation 1 positioned on the left-center. Goal is to place it to improve surface flatness and avoid creating holes while preserving flexibility for upcoming J,Z,L,I. From the current orientation, moving/locking immediately on the far left can fill the left shelf cleanly; a hard drop efficiently places it into that gap without extra risk. Other actions like rotating could create overhangs, and lateral movement is unnecessary before securing a decent placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top with an uneven but low stack below. The bottom surface has a left-side gap at column 0 on the bottom row and a shallow center-right structure; no immediate danger from height, so priority is efficient placement to keep the board flat and avoid sealing holes. The current J in spawn orientation can be moved and later rotated, but the strongest immediate move is to start shifting it left toward the open left side, where J can eventually tuck and help fill the left gap/step more cleanly than dropping from the center. Right would move away from the useful space, rotations are not yet necessary in open air, and dropping now would commit too early. Therefore move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the top with a flat, low stack overall and no immediate danger. Goal is to place it efficiently without creating awkward overhangs while keeping the center open for upcoming Z, L, I, T. Since the piece is still far from the stack, horizontal positioning matters first; moving right shifts the J toward the center-right where it can later rotate/drop into cleaner placements above the existing uneven surface. Hard/soft drop now would place it poorly on the left/top, and rotating before shifting is less urgent than reaching a better column.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near center in horizontal orientation. Surface below has a left-side stack and relatively open center/right, with no immediate danger since board is low. Goal now is clean placement that preserves a flat stack and flexibility for upcoming Z, L, I, T. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately from current centered position, likely leaving the J somewhere suboptimal without using horizontal movement; soft_drop advances but misses a chance to steer first; rotations are not urgently needed because J in current orientation is already useful for tucking later. Moving right heads toward the open middle-right well where J can be placed more cleanly on future turns, while left trends toward the existing left clutter. So the best single action now is to shift right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J is high and there is no immediate threat near the top, so the priority is to place it to improve the uneven lower stack. The bottom has a left-side well already occupied by I and some center bumps around O/T/S, while the far right is relatively open. A good sub-goal is to move the J toward the right so it can later tuck or stand against the right side without worsening the center. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock it awkwardly near the center-top with little control; soft_drop reduces flexibility too early; rotations are not yet necessary because current orientation can still be repositioned. Moving right preserves options and heads toward the cleaner landing zone.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current piece is a J near the top in spawn orientation. The stack is low and concentrated on the left/center, with a clear right side and no immediate threat. Goal now is to place J efficiently to keep the surface flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z,L,I,T. Since the piece is still high, the best immediate sub-goal is to start moving it toward an open landing zone rather than locking early. Among actions, left shifts it toward the center/left where it can potentially complement the existing left-side structure; right moves it farther into an already open but less immediately useful edge, rotations are premature before lateral positioning, and dropping now would commit too early. So the best action is to move left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high above a low uneven stack with open center/right space. Current threats are minimal since the piece is near the top and the surface below has a shallow valley around columns 4-6. Goal is to place J efficiently to keep the stack flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z, L, I, T. Immediate sub-goal: move the J toward the center-left so it can later drop into the central contour rather than staying stranded on the right. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it poorly on the high right and waste placement options; soft_drop commits downward too early before aligning; rotations are not yet necessary because current orientation is already useful and there is ample height; right moves away from the target area. Moving left starts positioning toward the central gap while retaining future rotate/drop options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board surface is low and uneven: left has L/O stack, center-right has a T/SS structure, and the active J is high above with plenty of time. Goal is to place J to improve the stack without creating awkward holes. The most promising immediate setup is to move the J toward the left side to potentially slot over/near the L-column area on subsequent turns; dropping now would leave it too central and floating, and rotating immediately is less urgent than horizontal alignment while high up. Among candidates, left advances toward that sub-goal best.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J is high above the stack with lots of horizontal freedom. The surface below has a left/middle buildup and a tall empty well on the right side, so immediate goal is to move the J toward a useful placement on the right without committing too early. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock it far from an optimized setup; soft_drop reduces flexibility while not yet aligned; rotations are not yet necessary because current horizontal J can still be shifted and rotated later. Moving right advances toward the open right side and preserves options for future rotations and drop timing.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a small uneven stack concentrated at the bottom. The active J is high near the center in horizontal orientation. Immediate threats are low since the stack is short, so the goal is to place J to improve the surface and avoid creating awkward overhangs. A good sub-goal is moving it toward the left gap/valley around columns 0-3 to support future Z, L, and I placements. Among candidate actions, hard_drop would commit too early from the current central position; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning. Rotating is not urgently needed because the current horizontal J can still be shifted into a useful lane. Moving left starts positioning toward the left-side terrain while preserving future rotation/drop options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high and centered. Surface below has a left-side cavity around columns 3-5 above the L/O/T stack, while the far right is cleaner but less immediately reachable in one move. Goal is to place J to support a flatter stack and avoid creating overhangs. A good near-term sub-goal is moving the J left toward the existing left structure so it can later slot in without blocking the center. Among actions, left shifts toward that target; right moves away, rotations don't yet exploit a landing spot, and dropping now would commit too early from a high position.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active J is high and unobstructed. The stack has a left-side structure with a 2-wide well at far left mostly open, and uneven center-right bumps around the S/T area. Immediate goal is to place J to improve surface and avoid sealing awkward gaps. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the J in its current column, likely too early without alignment; rotations could prepare a tuck but there is no urgency because piece is high. Moving left heads toward the open left side, where J can likely slot neatly against the existing L/O stack and help flatten the surface. Right would drift toward the taller, messier S/T side. Best sub-goal now is lateral positioning to the left before dropping.

Immediate goal is to place this L to improve surface flatness and avoid creating awkward overhangs while setting up for upcoming I/T/Z/J pieces. The current stack has useful terrain around columns 4-6 where an L in rotation 2 could eventually tuck to flatten the center-left area; rotating now is safe and preserves time to shift afterward. Hard/soft drop now would commit too early in a poor top placement, and horizontal moves before rotating may be less efficient. Best action: rotate_right.

The board’s surface has a notable left-side cavity/channel above the existing L/J structure, while the right side around the Z/S/T stack is bumpier and less immediately compatible. experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper-left with lots of empty space below before the existing stack starts around rows 12-19. The current stack has a shallow left-side structure and no immediate danger, so the goal is to place this L efficiently without creating overhangs. A hard drop now places the L in its current orientation onto the left side, likely settling around row 9-11 and starting to build a cleaner column/platform while preserving flexibility for upcoming I,T,Z,J pieces. Lateral moves or rotations are possible, but with so much vertical space, committing the piece downward now is the strongest progress toward stable stacking.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top spanning columns 3-6. The stack is low but uneven, with a deep left-side well partly filled and no immediate line clear available from the current position. Goal is to place the I for future efficiency without creating holes; the best immediate sub-goal is to stand it vertically in a relatively open column so it can descend cleanly and preserve the flatter center. Among actions, rotate_right converts the I to vertical at its current x-position in open space, enabling later drops into a useful lane. Moving first is less urgent than getting the beneficial orientation, and hard/soft drop horizontally now would waste the long bar across open air.

The immediate threat is low because the stack is only in the bottom half, so the best sub-goal is efficient placement for future line clears. A horizontal I can likely bridge or fill a 4-wide span better than the current vertical placement, and rotating now preserves flexibility before committing downward movement. Among actions, rotate_right changes the I from vertical to horizontal; left/right only shift without improving fit yet, and dropping now would commit to a less useful vertical column.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top at row 8, with a visible valid clockwise rotation to vertical in column 5. The stack below is still relatively low but uneven, with the bottom row almost full except for the far-left cell, and there is no immediate danger near the spawn area. My goal is to place this I piece to improve future line-clear potential rather than leave it floating high. Rotating now creates a vertical I that can be steered into a useful well on subsequent turns; dropping horizontally here would strand it across the upper board with poor value. Among candidates, rotate_right is best because it preserves flexibility and moves toward a strong placement, while left/right without rotating are less useful and dropping now is premature.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T near the top in spawn orientation. The stack is low-to-mid and uneven, with a notable vertical I column around x=5 and mixed cavities in the lower half. Immediate threat is low since the top is mostly empty. Goal now is to place the T to improve future fitting with upcoming Z, J, S, O rather than commit to a premature drop. A useful sub-goal is moving the T toward the center-left/left side where there are more contour opportunities, while avoiding the occupied I column on the right-center. Among actions, left begins repositioning toward likely landing spots; right moves into/over the I column area; rotations are not yet necessary with so much vertical space; dropping now would lock too early without horizontal alignment. So the best single action is to move left.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high near the top with plenty of horizontal freedom. The stack below has a notable cavity around the center-right columns above the lower T/OO structures, and with next pieces Z, J, S, O we want to keep the surface flexible rather than commit immediately. A hard or soft drop now in the current orientation would likely place the T flat without aligning to the central notch. Moving right this turn shifts the T toward the center-right where it can potentially be rotated into a useful tuck on the following move; moving left is less attractive because the left side is bumpier and more occupied. Rotation is not yet necessary because position is the main constraint while the piece is still high.

experiences.intentions: Active T piece is high near the center with plenty of lateral freedom. The stack has a notable vertical well/opening around column 4 above the lower center, and the current T in rotation 0 can likely be maneuvered to fit useful cavities later. Immediate goal is to position it toward the central-left structure rather than commit straight down from spawn. Candidate actions: hard_drop is premature from spawn and likely wastes placement high; soft_drop reduces maneuver time without benefit; rotations don't yet create a clearly superior shape at spawn; right moves away from the useful central gap. Moving left starts aligning the T with the central cavity and preserves future rotation/drop options.

There is no immediate danger at the top, so the goal is to improve placement rather than rush. A good sub-goal is to move the T toward the center-left where it can eventually fill notches and keep the middle flatter; dropping immediately from the spawn area would leave it floating inefficiently. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early in a poor central-top position; rotations are possible but not yet necessary before lateral alignment; right moves away from the better landing zone. Moving left starts positioning the T toward the useful cavity over the left stack while preserving future rotation options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T near the upper-left, with a mostly open field above uneven stacks. The immediate goal is to place it safely while preserving a flat surface and avoiding creating awkward holes. Current column heights suggest the left side has a cavity/step around columns 0-3, and moving the T right is not urgent because center-right already has a tall I column. Rotating to vertical could fit future niches, but from the current high spawn position the best first sub-goal is horizontal repositioning toward the left stack where a T can likely settle cleanly over the step. Among candidates, left advances toward that target; right moves toward taller clutter, rotation changes shape before aligning, and dropping now would commit too early from a poor x-position.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T near the upper left. The stack below has a central notch around columns 4-6 and the current T at spawn is far above it. Immediate threats are low since the board height is moderate, but preserving flexibility matters. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock the T in its current column placement, likely creating awkward surface shape on the left. soft_drop commits downward without lateral adjustment. Rotations here do not obviously improve future fit yet. Moving right starts guiding the T toward the center-right cavity where T placements are more useful and aligns with upcoming Z/J/S/O pieces. Goal now is to position the T for a cleaner surface and possible notch fill; sub-goal is horizontal alignment before dropping. Therefore choose right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top in horizontal orientation with open air below. Surface below has a left-center notch around columns 3-4 and more clutter on the right; immediate threat is low because piece is still high. Goal is to place J toward the left-center to complement the L/T structure and avoid stacking on the already busy right where Z/S blocks create bumps. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current central position, likely poor; soft_drop/no_op delay without improving alignment; rotations are not urgent since current J horizontal shape is useful; moving right worsens congestion. Moving left starts shifting toward the cleaner left-center landing zone and preserves future options.

The stack below is uneven, with a notable left-side structure around columns 1-3 and cleaner central-right lanes. My goal now is to position the J toward a useful landing that can smooth the left stack rather than commit immediately from high up. Among candidate actions, hard_drop would likely lock suboptimally with little adjustment; soft_drop reduces future flexibility; rotations are not yet necessary because the current orientation can still be moved into place. Moving left starts aligning the J with the left-side contours and preserves future options for rotation/drop on subsequent turns.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the upper left, currently floating with open space below. The stack is uneven but not near the top, so the immediate goal is to place this J to improve the left-side surface without creating extra holes. Moving left from the current position can tuck the J against the wall and set up a cleaner drop; rotating now is less urgent because the current orientation already works well for left-wall placement, and hard/soft drop immediately would leave it more central and less useful.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J near the upper left in rotation 0. The stack is uneven but not in immediate danger; the left side has a shallow notch and the center/right remain relatively open. Goal is to place the J cleanly without creating overhangs while preserving access for upcoming S,O,L,I. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current column, likely too early and less flexible; soft_drop is similarly premature. left is impossible or low value at the wall. right starts moving the J toward a cleaner landing zone over the left-center contours, and can still be rotated later. Rotating now near the wall changes shape but doesn’t yet exploit a clear slot as well as repositioning first. So the best immediate sub-goal is horizontal repositioning to the right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J near the upper-left. The stack is low and there is no immediate danger, so the goal is to place this J to improve the left surface without creating awkward holes. Current orientation already fits naturally above the left-side structure; moving or rotating now is less urgent than advancing it downward to preserve placement flexibility on later turns. Among candidates, soft_drop progresses toward locking a useful placement while still allowing future adjustment; hard_drop commits too early, and horizontal/rotation moves are not yet clearly better.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top, currently centered around columns 4-5. The stack below is uneven with a deep well on the far right and mixed bumps in the center-left. Immediate threat is low because the piece is high and board top is open. Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating overhangs, likely toward the right-center where existing Z/S structure can be complemented. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock an unsteered piece in a mediocre central position; soft_drop is premature before alignment; rotations are possible but not yet clearly best. Moving right improves access to the right-side contours and keeps future options for rotating or dropping next turn. So the best current sub-goal is lateral positioning to the right.

Rotating now is possible, but lateral movement first preserves both orientations and aims the piece toward a better landing zone.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the top, currently horizontal at columns 3-5 over a mostly uneven stack. The center-right has a vertical well around columns 4-5, while the left side is bumpier. Goal is to place the S cleanly without creating overhangs and set up for upcoming O/L/I pieces. From spawn, moving right would better align the horizontal S over the central notch; rotating creates a vertical S that doesn’t clearly fit yet, and dropping now would leave it awkwardly high on the left-center. So shifting right is the best immediate setup action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an S near the top with only one valid orientation at its current position; rotating would collide. The stack is highest on the left/middle and relatively lower on the far right, with a useful notch around columns 6-7 in the upper surface. Goal is to place the S cleanly without creating overhangs, and since it is already low enough to lock soon, the best immediate progress is to drop it into its current fitting channel. Left/right would require more setup and risk worse placement; rotation is invalid here. Hard dropping secures the piece efficiently in its current valid spot.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an O at the top centered around columns 3-4 with no rotation effect. The stack is uneven with a deep left-side well and mixed bumps; immediate threat is low because the top is mostly empty. Goal now is to place the O efficiently without creating extra holes, ideally using existing 2-wide depressions. Since rotation does nothing for O and hard drop immediately would likely strand it high on the center-left surface, shifting left first aims toward the cleaner 2-wide slot over columns 2-3/3-4 and preserves flexibility for later pieces. Other actions: right moves toward rougher terrain, soft_drop/hard_drop commit too early, no_op wastes a turn.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the top around columns 2-3. O has no meaningful rotation, and current stack shows a left-side notch above row 5 that could accommodate this O if moved left one and dropped. Goal is to place cleanly without creating overhangs; immediate sub-goal is to slide the O toward the left cavity before it descends. Candidate actions: left moves toward the useful slot, right moves away, rotations do nothing, soft/hard drop would commit too early on the current column. Best action is left.

experiences.intentions: The active piece appears to be the horizontal I on the bottom row at columns 2-5, resting on the floor with a one-cell gap at the far left. Immediate opportunities are limited because the board is already stable and the shown rotation does not change, suggesting the current placement is valid and near lock. My goal is to place this I to improve the surface and avoid creating extra holes. Moving left fills the lone bottom-left gap with the I spanning columns 1-4, which leaves a cleaner edge than staying or moving right and is better than rotating here. Hard dropping would just lock in the current less-optimal position. So the best single action now is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active L is near the top with plenty of horizontal space; the stack below is uneven but not threatening an immediate top-out. Current goal is to place the L to improve surface and avoid creating overhangs while keeping future I/S/Z/T placements flexible. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would commit the current flat orientation high-level without alignment; left/right can still position, but a useful first step is rotating to a vertical L so it can fit columns better and preserve options on subsequent moves. Rotation 1 gives a compact vertical shape without collision and is the best setup move now.

experiences.intentions: Key state: active L is high near the top in vertical-right orientation. Surface below has a notable left-center cavity around columns 3-4 near rows 1-3 from the top, while the center-right stack is already uneven. Immediate threat is low because piece is near spawn with space, but we want to place it efficiently to reduce holes and set up upcoming I/S/Z/T pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit to the current column 5 placement, likely creating awkward overhangs on the right. Soft_drop also commits downward before improving alignment. Rotating could help, but first shifting left targets the visible left-center notch where an L can fit more naturally; moving right worsens the crowded right side. Sub-goal now is reposition horizontally before dropping. Best action: left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L in rotation 1, currently near columns 4-5 at the top. The stack below is uneven with a notable left-side cavity around row 4 and generally more open space under/left of the spawn area than to the right. Goal is to place this L efficiently without creating extra overhangs, while setting up upcoming I/S/Z/T. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit a vertical L high and awkwardly; soft_drop gives less flexibility than shifting first; rotating can create a flatter shape but immediate horizontal adjustment is more valuable before gravity. Moving left heads toward the accessible notch and broader landing options, improving future placement. Moving right trends into a more cluttered central-right area. So the best immediate sub-goal is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L in rotation 1, sitting high with open space below. The stack is uneven but there is a notable left-side cavity/slot around columns 2-4 near the upper surface that matches an L laid flat better than the current vertical pose. Goal is to improve surface flatness and avoid creating overhangs while setting up upcoming I/S/Z/T pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit the current awkward vertical orientation; soft_drop also advances without improving fit. Moving left/right first may help later, but the immediate strongest improvement is to rotate into rotation 2, which places the L horizontally and is likely to slot more cleanly on the left plateau. rotate_left from current rotation 1 goes to rotation 0, while rotate_right goes to rotation 2. So rotate_right is best.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is L at the upper-left resting above a cluttered stack. Current orientation (rotation 2) is already placed over columns 0-2 with overhang; rotation 3 would shift it into a cleaner vertical-left shape fitting snugly near the left wall without creating as much horizontal overhang. Rotation 1 is invalid, and immediate drops in current orientation risk a messy surface. Goal is to keep the stack flat and avoid holes while setting up future I/S/Z/T pieces. Among actions, rotate_right changes to the more useful rotation 3; left/right are constrained near wall, and dropping now before improving orientation is worse.

The stack is uneven with no immediate line clear available from a horizontal hard drop, while a vertical I can be steered into a useful well on later moves. Goal is to improve placement flexibility and avoid wasting the I flat on the high surface. Among actions, rotate_right is best now because it converts the piece to vertical while still high enough to reposition on subsequent turns; left/right alone keep it horizontal, and dropping now would commit prematurely.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I in vertical orientation near column 5 with space below to continue descending. The stack is uneven but there is no immediate line clear available by dropping now; preserving flexibility matters. Goal is to place the I to support future clears without creating overhangs. Candidate actions: left/right would reposition but need more context and time; rotation to horizontal at this height would float over gaps and is less urgent. Hard drop commits too early. Soft drop safely advances the piece downward while keeping future movement and rotation options open.
